methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search identified the official UPL AI for learning hub and its examination, academic writing, teaching, and student learning environment subpages.
Firecrawl search found a formal Umeå University IT strategy PDF with a specific section on AI services, plus research projects and reports rejected as low-specificity policy sources.
Verified all retained sources as Umeå University-controlled pages or PDFs under www.umu.se.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found Umeå University's UPL AI for learning pages, the student-facing cheating and plagiarism page with an AI examination warning, and a formal IT strategy PDF section on AI services. No central binding university-wide generative-AI teaching policy was found; extracted claims are scoped to guidance, development work, academic-integrity warning text, and IT strategy requirements.

artifacts.claimText: Umeå University's UPL has initiated a three-year AI for learning initiative intended to build a university-wide platform for knowledge development, exchange of experience, and collegial learning.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Umeå University's AI and examination work area is developing principles, examples, and working methods for sustainable examination practice, including transparent and responsible use of AI as a learning tool.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Umeå University's UPL academic-writing work area frames generative AI as both a possible support for students' writing and a source of challenges around metacognition, dependence on language models, and academic integrity.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Umeå University's UPL AI and teaching page says the work area focuses on teachers' didactic choices and professional judgment, including how AI is understood, valued, used, or deliberately not used in relation to teaching purposes.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Umeå University's student cheating and plagiarism page warns that using AI or similar services in examination to produce text and submitting it as one's own without openly disclosing how the material was created can amount to attempted misleading.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Umeå University's IT strategy says university-wide AI services have undergone necessary controls, and that AI services should be managed like other IT services under the university's system or cloud-service acquisition instructions.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

Approved with development-work wording. Source does not impose mandatory examination rules.
Approved as scoped UPL academic-writing guidance; does not assert a rule for all coursework.
Approved as a teaching-guidance claim about didactic decision-making.
Approved because the student page itself says undisclosed AI-created examination text can constitute attempted misleading; wording preserves the conditional 'can'.
Approved as an IT strategy claim. Mandatory language is limited to the PDF's statement that AI services shall be handled like other IT services.

artifacts.title: Umeå University AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: The staged crawl found official Umeå University evidence for a UPL AI for learning initiative, UPL guidance-development work around examination, academic writing, and teaching, a student-facing warning that undisclosed AI-created examination material can be misleading, and a formal IT strategy section requiring controls and IT-service handling for AI services. No central binding university-wide generative-AI teaching policy was found in public official sources.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No central binding university-wide generative-AI teaching policy was found in public official sources during this staged crawl.
UPL pages are treated as guidance and development-work evidence, not binding rules.
Course pages, research project pages, and library-news items were rejected as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
